perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be for a period of five years beginning on the date hereof; provided that the obligations relating to Confidential Information disclosed during the term of this Agreement shall survive the expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall be for a period of five years beginning on the date hereof; provided that the obligations relating to Confidential Information disclosed during the term of this Agreement shall survive the expiration of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may terminate this agreement at any time with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating the agreement is for five years and then stating it can be terminated at any time with 30 days' notice. This contradicts the five-year term outlined earlier in the same section because either party may terminate the agreement earlier than the 5-year period. This creates uncertainty and potential disputes about the actual duration of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement shall be for a period of five years beginning on the date hereof; provided that the obligations relating to Confidential Information disclosed during the term of this Agreement shall survive the expiration of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may terminate this agreement at any time with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text specifies a five-year term for the agreement. The modification allows either party to terminate the agreement at any time with 30 days' notice. This directly contradicts the initial five-year term because it permits termination before the five-year period elapses. The contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text and the Explanation is valid. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: All right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing party and the Confidential Information shall be held in trust and confidence by the receiving party for the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: All right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing party and the Confidential Information shall be held in trust and confidence by the receiving party for the disclosing party. However, after two years from the disclosure, the receiving party shall have the right to use the Confidential Information for internal research and development purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates a contradiction by initially stating that all rights to the Confidential Information remain with the disclosing party. It then introduces an exception allowing the receiving party to use the information for internal R&D after two years. This contradicts the original statement of exclusive ownership and introduces uncertainty about the scope of the disclosing party's rights, specifically in the situation where after 2 years, the recieving party has rights over the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing party and the Confidential Information shall be held in trust and confidence by the receiving party for the disclosing party. However, after two years from the disclosure, the receiving party shall have the right to use the Confidential Information for internal research and development purposes.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that all rights remain with the disclosing party, but then allows the receiving party to use the information after two years for internal R&D. This contradicts the initial statement of exclusive ownership and introduces uncertainty about the scope of the disclosing party's rights. The Contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text. The original text states exclusive ownership. The addition in the Changed Text creates an exception after 2 years, which is a contradiction as after 2 years the recieving party now has rights over confidential information. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information shall not be copied, reproduced in any form or stored in a retrieval system or data base by the receiving party without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, except for such copies and storage as may reasonably be required internally by the receiving party in connection with the Opportunity.
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidential Information shall not be copied, reproduced in any form or stored in a retrieval system or data base by the receiving party without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, except for such copies and storage as may reasonably be required internally by the receiving party in connection with the Opportunity. The receiving party may, however, create derivative works based on the Confidential Information without the disclosing party’s consent, provided such derivative works are solely for internal use.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original restriction on copying or reproducing Confidential Information without consent. While the original text allows for internal copies related to the Opportunity, the added clause permits the creation of derivative works (which inherently involve copying and reproduction) for internal use without consent. This in-text contradiction introduces ambiguity about what actions the receiving party can legally take regarding the Confidential Information, creating the possibility of conflicts between the 2 parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Confidential Information shall not be copied, reproduced in any form or stored in a retrieval system or data base by the receiving party without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, except for such copies and storage as may reasonably be required internally by the receiving party in connection with the Opportunity. The receiving party may, however, create derivative works based on the Confidential Information without the disclosing party’s consent, provided such derivative works are solely for internal use.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause permitting the creation of derivative works without the disclosing party's consent, even though the original text, which is still present, prohibits copying or reproducing Confidential Information without prior written consent. This directly contradicts the initial restriction, creating an in-text inconsistency as defined by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party shall not directly or indirectly contact any shareholders, officers, employees, affiliates, agents, lending institutions, customers or suppliers of the disclosing party in connection with a possible transaction involving the disclosing party without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party shall not directly or indirectly contact any shareholders, officers, employees, affiliates, agents, lending institutions, customers or suppliers of the disclosing party in connection with a possible transaction involving the disclosing party without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld. However, the receiving party is permitted to contact the disclosing party's customers for market research purposes related to the Opportunity, without obtaining prior consent.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction. The original clause restricts contact with any of the disclosing party's stakeholders without prior consent. The addition allows direct contact with customers for market research without consent. This creates an in-text contradiction within this section on when is it appropriate for the recieving party, to reach out to the disclosing parties. Specifically, the customers can be contacted, without the need of the disclosing party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party shall not directly or indirectly contact any shareholders, officers, employees, affiliates, agents, lending institutions, customers or suppliers of the disclosing party in connection with a possible transaction involving the disclosing party without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld. However, the receiving party is permitted to contact the disclosing party's customers for market research purposes related to the Opportunity, without obtaining prior consent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original clause restricts contact with any of the disclosing party's stakeholders without prior consent. The addition allows direct contact with customers for market research without consent, which is a direct contradiction in the same clause.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements and understandings, express and implied, oral and written.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements and understandings, express and implied, oral and written. However, any oral agreements made prior to the signing of this document regarding marketing strategies shall remain in effect.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by contradicting the entire agreement clause. The original clause states that the written agreement supersedes all prior agreements, both written and oral. The new addition states that prior oral agreements regarding marketing strategies remain in effect. This creates uncertainty about which agreements govern the parties' relationship and can lead to disputes about what terms are enforceable, specifically marketing strategies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements and understandings, express and implied, oral and written. However, any oral agreements made prior to the signing of this document regarding marketing strategies shall remain in effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The original clause states the agreement supersedes all prior agreements. The added text says that prior oral agreements regarding marketing strategies remain in effect. This is a direct and strong contradiction, because the 'entire understanding' clause is rendered meaningless by the explicit exception. The Contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text, but this is because the changed text introduced the contradiction.
